editionData.title: July 21, 2026: Nearly 100 U.S. troops injured in last two weeks of Iran war, Pentagon says; A U.K. prime minister takes charge promising to empower...
editionData.description: Global headlines: Nearly 100 U.S. troops injured in last two weeks of Iran war, Pentagon says (An intensifying cycle of violence has left at least three U.S. service members dead since the resumption of hostilities a week ago); A U.K. prime minister takes charge promising to empower local officials (Andy Burnham, until recently the mayor of Greater Manchester, promised to create a “circuit breaker” moment in the cycle of failure and cynicism that have plagued British politics for the past decade); and Ukraine bombs warehouses, targeting Wildberries — Russia’s version of Amazon (Ukraine’s long-range drone attacks, including strikes this weekend on e-commerce warehouses, have undercut the Kremlin’s efforts to shield most Russian citizens from the war). Markets leaned lower, with 5 of 7 tracked indexes down; Nikkei 225 up 2.67% was the largest move. Benchmarks tracked: S&P 500, NASDAQ Composite, Euronext 100 Index, and Public Joint-Stock Company Moscow Exchange MICEX-RTS plus 3 more indexes. Hacker News: Jane Street: Incremental (A library for incremental computations. Contribute to janestreet/incremental development by creating an account on GitHub); Who's afraid of Chinese models? (Everyone is worried about Chinese models, but the frontier labs will be fine; we need to enable open U.S. alternatives); and Running Doom on Our Custom CPU and Going Viral (I get kidnapped by the MIG while adding DDR3 to our CPU). Product Hunt features Autoplot (Unify data analysis and plotting in one powerful workspace), CaptureKit (Keep your desktop clean by auto-organizing screenshots), and BlockscopeChat (AI Crypto Investigations/Research Agents).
editionData.author: Sony Mathew

headlines.title: Nearly 100 U.S. troops injured in last two weeks of Iran war, Pentagon says
headlines.summary: An intensifying cycle of violence has left at least three U.S. service members dead since the resumption of hostilities a week ago.

headlines.title: A U.K. prime minister takes charge promising to empower local officials
headlines.summary: Andy Burnham, until recently the mayor of Greater Manchester, promised to create a “circuit breaker” moment in the cycle of failure and cynicism that have plagued British politics for the past decade.

headlines.title: Ukraine bombs warehouses, targeting Wildberries — Russia’s version of Amazon
headlines.summary: Ukraine’s long-range drone attacks, including strikes this weekend on e-commerce warehouses, have undercut the Kremlin’s efforts to shield most Russian citizens from the war.

headlines.title: U.S. strikes unlikely to move Iran, intelligence reports say
headlines.summary: American agencies see a long standoff between Tehran and Washington, as Iran is unlikely to soften its negotiating position because of U.S. military action.

headlines.title: Sinaloa cartel leader ‘El Mayo’ Zambada sentenced to life without parole
headlines.summary: Ismael “El Mayo” Zambada co-founded Mexico’s Sinaloa cartel with Joaquín “El Chapo” Guzmán and led the criminal organization after Guzmán‘s 2016 capture.

headlines.title: China and Philippines trade accusations after sailor injured at sea
headlines.summary: Dispute erupts in South China Sea after incident near Second Thomas Shoal involving Chinese and Philippine vessels.

headlines.title: Lebanese return to ‘pilot zone’ villages they say Israel never occupied
headlines.summary: Under a US-brokered plan, Israel is to hand over control of some areas it occupies in Lebanon to Lebanese government.

headlines.title: Who is driving diplomacy in the Horn of Africa and the Red Sea, and why?
headlines.summary: A series of diplomatic consultations in Cairo underscores the Horn of Africa's growing strategic importance.

headlines.title: Guyana recovers 27 bodies after ferry disaster as crew comes under scrutiny
headlines.summary: Authorities detain ferry crew members after drug tests reveal violations in Guyana's worst marine disaster in decades.

headlines.title: Trump imposes 50% US tariffs on some Canadian goods, citing discrimination
headlines.summary: Tariffs apply to Canadian wine, hockey sticks, cement, and other products, sparking fears of escalating trade tensions.

headlines.title: FDA still focused on Taylor Farms lettuce as Cyclospora source despite retracted test
headlines.summary: The FDA says lettuce distributed by Taylor Fresh Foods and Taylor Farms de Mexico is still linked to the multistate outbreak of cyclosporiasis. But the source may be difficult to definitively trace.

headlines.title: At Mississippi funeral, Civil Rights leaders call for justice for Nolan Wells, 18
headlines.summary: The parents of Nolan Wells, who was found dead on Horn Island in Mississippi, held a funeral for the 18-year-old on Monday. Wells was with friends for the Fourth of July and did not return home.

headlines.title: World Cup: Spain's homecoming heroes
headlines.summary: A nation celebrates as Spain's World Cup champions come home to a hero's welcome.

headlines.title: 10 things that wowed us at the 2026 World Cup
headlines.summary: The United States, Mexico, and Canada co-hosted the nearly six-week tournament. NPR's team of journalists who covered the World Cup reflects on the experience.

headlines.title: Court ruling freezes Paramount-Warner Bros. merger for now
headlines.summary: Paramount's owners were hoping they'd be firmly in control of rival Warner Bros. Discovery by the end of this week. Monday's temporary restraining order puts that plan on hold.

headlines.title: Kenya targets bigger tea exports to Chinese market
headlines.summary: Kenya is seeking to deepen cooperation with China in tea production, technology transfer and market access as it positions itself to expand exports to the world's largest tea-consuming nation, the Tea Board of Kenya says.

headlines.title: Burnham vows stability as he takes office as UK's PM
headlines.summary: The United Kingdom's new prime minister — the country's seventh leader in just over a decade — started his time in office on Monday by promising much-needed stability.

headlines.title: ASEAN ministers gather amid rising geopolitical challenges
headlines.summary: The Association of Southeast Asian Nations can serve as a "strategic shock absorber" in an era of intensifying global tensions, with the foreign ministers gathering in Manila this week expected to navigate widening geopolitical rivalry, according to analysts.

headlines.title: Beijing, Bangkok to forge closer relations
headlines.summary: China and Thailand signed on Monday multiple cooperation documents in the fields of trade, agriculture, customs, science and technology, artificial intelligence education, aviation and aerospace, intellectual property, media, and combating transnational crimes, as part of their efforts to enhance bilateral ties.

headlines.title: Japan warned against changing non-nuclear principles
headlines.summary: China on Monday urged the Japanese authorities not to defy public opinion or persist in what it described as a dangerous push toward remilitarization and nuclear armament.

headlines.title: Supreme Court proposes SIT probe into Ram Temple case
headlines.summary: Supreme Court proposes SIT investigation into Ram Temple donation embezzlement, urging petitioners to avoid politicisation of the case.

headlines.title: Modi is India’s most ‘anti-youth PM’, says Rahul Gandhi
headlines.summary: Rahul Gandhi labels Narendra Modi as India's "most anti-youth" PM, criticizing the government's response to students' educational concerns.

headlines.title: Lok Sabha proceedings disrupted on first day of Monsoon Session
headlines.summary: Lok Sabha proceedings faced disruptions on the Monsoon Session's first day due to Opposition protests over NEET leaks and Ram temple donations.

headlines.title: CJP protest in Delhi LIVE: Protesters reclaim Jantar Mantar protest site; Wangchuk to continue indefinite fast
headlines.summary: CJP protest: Jantar Mantar site reclaimed, Sonam Wangchuk to continue indefinite fast

headlines.title: Haryana police close Shambu border ahead of farmers’ motorcycle rally
headlines.summary: Haryana police close Shambu border ahead of farmers' motorcycle rally; traffic advisory issued with alternative routes available.

headlines.title: Asian stocks rise as Mideast mediation takes oil lower
headlines.summary: Reuters: Asian stocks rise as Mideast mediation takes oil lower.

headlines.title: Samsung Electronics creates robotics division; ex-Hyundai executive to head strategy
headlines.summary: Samsung Electronics said on Tuesday it ​will set up a robotics division reporting directly ‌to the chief executive to accelerate development and commercialisation in the field and establish robotics as a growth engine.

headlines.title: Japan protests Chinese live-fire drill in nearby waters
headlines.summary: Japan said on Tuesday it had lodged a protest with China about ​live-fire drills it says were conducted in its exclusive economic zone, an ‌incident that could further strain relations between Asia's top two economies.

headlines.title: US judge approves Anthropic's $1.5 billion settlement of copyright lawsuit
headlines.summary: Reuters: US judge approves Anthropic's $1.5 billion settlement of copyright lawsuit.

headlines.title: Darline Graham to run for full term to replace her brother in US Senate
headlines.summary: U.S. Senator Darline Graham said on Monday she plans to run for a full ​term to replace her brother, late Senator Lindsey Graham, who died this ‌month after serving two decades in the U.S. Senate.

markets.label: Public Joint-Stock Company Moscow Exchange MICEX-RTS

hackerNews.summary: Everyone is worried about Chinese models, but the frontier labs will be fine; we need to enable open U.S. alternatives.

hackerNews.title: A Koi Pond Mosaic Made from 10 Pounds of 3D Printer Waste
hackerNews.summary: A Koi Pond Mosaic Made From 10 Pounds of 3D Printer Waste: Every 3D printer owner ends up with quite a pile of plastic waste: failed prints, stringy purge, and the plastic purge tower left behind after a multicolor job. Over three years I saved every scrap of mine, and it added up. When I finally weighed it…

hackerNews.title: Five US tech giants' hidden debts soar to $1.65T on opaque AI funding
hackerNews.summary: Data center leases, GPU supply contracts raise liabilities at Meta, Oracle, Nikkei study shows

hackerNews.title: Kimi Work
hackerNews.summary: Download the desktop AI to automate workflows, organize files, & operate across the web. Empower financial research, analysis, & office tasks with 300 agents.

hackerNews.title: Jelly UI: Soft-body physics for native HTML form controls
hackerNews.summary: Jelly UI is a dependency-free Web Components library for tactile product interfaces: soft-body physics, real form controls, dark mode, RTL and WCAG AA accessibility.

hackerNews.title: Human mathematicians are being outcounterexampled
hackerNews.summary: It's been an interesting few weeks for counterexamples. This post is basically my perspective of what has been going on in the world of formalization, AI tools and, in particular, counterexam…

hackerNews.title: Show HN: Ex Situ – open-source spatial index of displaced cultural artifacts
hackerNews.summary: A spatial index of displaced cultural artifacts — mapped from origin site to holding institution.

hackerNews.title: Jellyfin founder Andrew leaves team
hackerNews.summary: Hello everyone. Effective yesterday, Anthony and I decided to depart from the project, myself as Project Leader, and he as a core team member. This is in addition to the resignation of Andrew on Frida
